title: Executive Order 12738—Administration of Foreign Assistance and Related Functions and Arms Export Controls

flags.justification: Routine maintenance of an existing comprehensive delegation framework (EO 12163) to reflect newly-enacted appropriations legislation, with several provisions explicitly requiring consultation with the Secretary of State, Treasury, or Defense.
Extreme, precise specificity throughout, with exact statutory section citations for every amendment.
Many distinct provision-updates all coherently serve one purpose: updating an existing delegation framework to reflect new appropriations legislation.
Internal delegation-authority updates, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing delegation framework implementing permanent statutory authority.
Multiple provisions add rather than remove 'in consultation with' requirements across several named officials.
An internal delegation-maintenance order with no third-party compliance regime.
This updates an existing, comprehensive delegation framework rather than creating new authority.
Extensive cross-referencing to specific officials with defined consultation requirements, well-coordinated despite complexity.
No new exemption category is created; this updates who exercises existing delegated authority.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. An extremely dense but well-designed maintenance amendment to a comprehensive foreign-assistance delegation framework, with real, recurring cross-agency consultation requirements built throughout.
